steps.title: Understand the units
steps.content: The given rate is $1{,}650$ meters per hour per second, i.e. $1{,}650\ \tfrac{\text{m/h}}{\text{s}}$. We want feet per second squared, $\tfrac{\text{ft/s}}{\text{s}}=\text{ft/s}^2$. We need to convert meters to feet and hours to seconds.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A ($0.14$): Off by a factor of about $10$; this would result from dividing by $36000$ instead of $3600$. Eliminated.
  • B ($1.50$): Matches the computed value $\approx 1.503$ ft/s$^2$.
  • C ($8.38$): Too large by a factor of about $5.6$. Eliminated.
  • D ($90.2$): Far too large; would come from forgetting to divide by $3600$. Eliminated.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
